mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive forward returns occur in less than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8 percent, labor market deterioration signals severe economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable consumer prices indicate robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative mean forward return or a Rank IC below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which immediately boosts equity valuations over the following week during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when INDPRO increases during expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as economic momentum builds.
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative average forward return or fails to achieve statistical significance at the 90% confidence level over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in consumer prices triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, driving positive S&P 500 returns as wage growth supports consumer spending.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces a negative mean forward return or a Rank IC below 0.02 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 2 percent confirms macroeconomic expansion, which sustains corporate earnings trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2 percent.
